title: Audit skills against Jan 18 baseline for stale CLI references
description: After reverting orch-go to Jan 18 baseline (0bca3dec), skills in orch-knowledge may reference CLI commands, flags, or features that don't exist. We fixed orch phase -> bd comment, but need a comprehensive audit. Check all 20 skills for references to: orch lint, state.db, worktree isolation, any post-Jan-18 orch subcommands. Also fix the orch-knowledge pre-commit hook (references orch lint --skills which doesn't exist at Jan 18).

title: Audit entropy spiral for recoverable features by functional area
description:
## Context
Reverted orch-go to Jan 18 baseline (0bca3dec) after entropy spiral (1163 agent commits, 5.4M LOC churn). Evidence preserved on entropy-spiral-feb2026 branch. Some features built during the spiral were genuinely valuable and should be recovered.

## Approach
Diff Jan 18 (0bca3dec) vs entropy-spiral-feb2026 by functional area. For each area, identify:
- Net-new features (not just churn/refactor cycles)
- Whether the feature compiled/worked at HEAD of spiral branch
- Dependencies on other spiral-era code
- Recovery complexity (clean extract vs needs adaptation)

## Areas to audit
1. **Web UI** - /work-graph route, dashboard agent cards, vite config changes
2. **Spawn system** - Backend abstraction (pkg/spawn/backends/), new flags, spawn pipeline
3. **Completion pipeline** - Pipeline decomposition, skip config, retry logic, SkipConfig type
4. **Daemon** - ProcessedIssueCache, skill inference improvements, processed-cache
5. **Observability** - SSE improvements, attention system (pkg/attention/), churn metrics
6. **Knowledge system** - Models/probes Go code (already recovering separately), kb reflect
7. **CLI commands** - Any new subcommands that were useful (orch phase, orch lint, etc.)

## Deliverables
- Recovery priority list: which features to recover first based on value vs complexity
- For each recommended recovery: exact commits/files to cherry-pick
- Flag features that depend on each other (can't recover X without Y)

## Method
Use git diff, git log, and file analysis on the entropy-spiral-feb2026 branch.
Do NOT modify any code - this is a read-only audit.

title: Cherry-pick spawn backends abstraction from entropy spiral
description: Recovery from entropy spiral. Cherry-pick pkg/spawn/backends/ (6 files) from commit 40d09539 on entropy-spiral-feb2026 branch. Self-contained abstraction for spawn backends (claude, opencode). LOW complexity. Use git show to extract, do NOT checkout the spiral branch. Verify it compiles against Jan 18 baseline spawn_cmd.go.

comments.author: dylanconlin
comments.text: Phase: Planning - Reading spawn backends files from entropy-spiral branch via git show

title: Cherry-pick verification spec generation from entropy spiral
description: Recovery from entropy spiral. Cherry-pick pkg/spawn/verification_spec.go + test file from entropy-spiral-feb2026 branch. Self-contained module for generating verification specs at spawn time. LOW complexity. Use git show to extract, do NOT checkout the spiral branch.

comments.author: dylanconlin

title: Recover skill inference from entropy spiral
description: Recovery from entropy spiral. Cherry-pick pkg/daemon/skill_inference.go from entropy-spiral-feb2026 branch. Daemon uses this to infer which skill to spawn for a given beads issue type. LOW complexity. Use git show to extract, do NOT checkout the spiral branch.

title: Recover attention system from entropy spiral
description: Recovery from entropy spiral. The pkg/attention/ package was built during the spiral (18 commits from 654dbe72 to 6766baaa), then accidentally wiped by a bd sync commit (3b004bef). HIGH complexity — needs extraction from pre-3b004bef commits, not branch HEAD. The attention system provides signal collectors (stuck, unblocked, stale, recently-closed, verify-failed, etc.) that feed the work graph dashboard. Use git show on commits before 3b004bef to extract. Includes cmd/orch/serve_attention.go API endpoint and web/src/lib/stores/attention.ts frontend.

title: Recover dashboard/web UI from entropy spiral
description: Recovery from entropy spiral. The web/ directory contains a SvelteKit dashboard with work graph, agent status cards, and attention surface. MEDIUM complexity — depends on attention system and other pkg/ changes. Lower priority, recover after core packages. Use git show on entropy-spiral-feb2026 to assess what's extractable.

title: Recover daemon rate limiting from entropy spiral
description: Recovery from entropy spiral. Cherry-pick rate limiting logic from pkg/daemon/daemon.go on entropy-spiral-feb2026 branch. LOW complexity. Prevents daemon from spawning too many agents too quickly. Use git show to extract relevant functions.

title: Guard bd sync against bulk file commits
description: bd sync does a bulk git add + commit that sweeps in whatever state the worktree is in, with no awareness of what SHOULD be committed. On a clean worktree this is fine (Jan 18 bd syncs only touched .beads/ and .kb/quick/), but in a dirty worktree it becomes destructive. Fix: bd sync should limit its git add scope to .beads/ and .kb/quick/ files only, never git add -A. Or add a safety check: if staged changes touch >N files or include non-beads paths, abort with a warning.
repro: During entropy spiral, bd sync commit 3b004bef touched 6,785 files and deleted entire packages (pkg/attention/, pkg/action/, etc.) because worktree was dirty with agent deletions
